title: Label reference
weight: 143
---

<!-- Code generated by cmd/labelsgen ("go generate ./..."); DO NOT EDIT. -->
<!-- Regenerate with `make labels-docs` after changing pkg/sablier/labels.go. -->

Sablier discovers and manages your workloads through these `sablier.*` labels. Provider-specific setup (Kubernetes annotations, Proxmox tags, Docker-only features) is noted per label and covered in depth on the linked feature pages. See the [compatibility matrix](/compatibility/) for an at-a-glance provider grid.

## `sablier.enable`

Opts the instance into Sablier management. Any value other than `true` is ignored.

- **Type:** boolean
- **Example:** `"true"`
- **Required:** yes
- **Since:** [v1.4.0](https://github.com/sablierapp/sablier/releases/tag/v1.4.0)
- **Providers:** Kubernetes must set this as a **label** (discovery uses a label selector), never an annotation. Proxmox LXC uses the `sablier` tag instead.

## `sablier.group`

Assigns the instance to one or more named groups. A session for any of its groups starts the instance.

- **Type:** comma-separated list
- **Default:** "default"
- **Example:** `"team-a,team-b"`
- **Since:** [v1.4.0](https://github.com/sablierapp/sablier/releases/tag/v1.4.0)
- **Providers:** Kubernetes must set a multi-value list as an **annotation**. Proxmox LXC uses one `sablier-group-<name>` tag per group.
- **Learn more:** [feature page](/features/multiple-groups/)

## `sablier.ready-after`

The minimum settling delay after the instance first reports ready before Sablier treats it as ready.

- **Type:** Go duration
- **Example:** `"30s"`
- **Since:** [v1.13.0](https://github.com/sablierapp/sablier/releases/tag/v1.13.0)
- **Learn more:** [feature page](/features/ready-after/)

## `sablier.ready-on-start`

Treats the instance as ready as soon as the start is dispatched, skipping the health check.

- **Type:** boolean
- **Example:** `"true"`
- **Since:** unreleased
- **Learn more:** [feature page](/features/ready-on-start/)

## `sablier.running-hours`

A daily keep-warm window in local time. Overnight windows like `22:00-06:00` span midnight.

- **Type:** `HH:MM-HH:MM`
- **Example:** `"09:00-18:00"`
- **Since:** [v1.13.0](https://github.com/sablierapp/sablier/releases/tag/v1.13.0)
- **Providers:** Kubernetes must set the colon value as an **annotation**.
- **Learn more:** [feature page](/features/running-hours/)

## `sablier.running-days`

Restricts the `sablier.running-hours` window to specific weekdays.

- **Type:** comma-separated weekdays
- **Default:** every day
- **Example:** `"Mon,Tue,Wed,Thu,Fri"`
- **Since:** unreleased
- **Providers:** Kubernetes must set the comma value as an **annotation**.
- **Learn more:** [feature page](/features/running-hours/)

## `sablier.anti-affinity`

Lists the group names this instance backs off from; it is forced idle while any listed group has an active session.

- **Type:** comma-separated list
- **Example:** `"streaming"`
- **Since:** unreleased
- **Providers:** Kubernetes must set a multi-value list as an **annotation**. Not supported on Proxmox LXC.
- **Learn more:** [feature page](/features/anti-affinity/)

## `sablier.idle.replicas`

The replica count when idle. `0` stops the workload; `1` or more keeps it running with optional resource throttling.

- **Type:** integer
- **Default:** `0` (stop)
- **Example:** `"1"`
- **Since:** [v1.13.0](https://github.com/sablierapp/sablier/releases/tag/v1.13.0)
- **Providers:** Not supported on Proxmox LXC.
- **Learn more:** [feature page](/features/scale-mode/)

## `sablier.idle.cpu`

The CPU limit applied when the session expires. Requires `sablier.idle.replicas >= 1`.

- **Type:** decimal cores / Kubernetes quantity
- **Example:** `"0.1"`
- **Since:** [v1.13.0](https://github.com/sablierapp/sablier/releases/tag/v1.13.0)
- **Providers:** Kubernetes uses a resource quantity (e.g. `100m`). Not supported on Proxmox LXC.
- **Learn more:** [feature page](/features/scale-mode/cpu/)

## `sablier.idle.memory`

The memory limit applied when the session expires. Requires `sablier.idle.replicas >= 1`.

- **Type:** Docker units / Kubernetes quantity
- **Example:** `"128m"`
- **Since:** [v1.13.0](https://github.com/sablierapp/sablier/releases/tag/v1.13.0)
- **Providers:** Kubernetes uses a resource quantity (e.g. `128Mi`). Not supported on Proxmox LXC.
- **Learn more:** [feature page](/features/scale-mode/memory/)

## `sablier.active.replicas`

The replica count restored when a new session is requested.

- **Type:** integer
- **Default:** `1`
- **Example:** `"2"`
- **Since:** [v1.13.0](https://github.com/sablierapp/sablier/releases/tag/v1.13.0)
- **Providers:** Not supported on Proxmox LXC.
- **Learn more:** [feature page](/features/scale-mode/)

## `sablier.active.cpu`

The CPU limit restored when a new session is requested.

- **Type:** decimal cores / Kubernetes quantity
- **Example:** `"2.0"`
- **Since:** [v1.13.0](https://github.com/sablierapp/sablier/releases/tag/v1.13.0)
- **Providers:** Kubernetes uses a resource quantity (e.g. `2000m`). Not supported on Proxmox LXC.
- **Learn more:** [feature page](/features/scale-mode/cpu/)

## `sablier.active.memory`

The memory limit restored when a new session is requested.

- **Type:** Docker units / Kubernetes quantity
- **Example:** `"512m"`
- **Since:** [v1.13.0](https://github.com/sablierapp/sablier/releases/tag/v1.13.0)
- **Providers:** Kubernetes uses a resource quantity (e.g. `512Mi`). Not supported on Proxmox LXC.
- **Learn more:** [feature page](/features/scale-mode/memory/)

## `sablier.idle.blkio-weight`

The relative block I/O scheduling weight applied when idle.

- **Type:** integer `10`-`1000`
- **Example:** `"100"`
- **Since:** unreleased
- **Providers:** Docker and Podman only.
- **Learn more:** [feature page](/features/scale-mode/block-io/)

## `sablier.active.blkio-weight`

The relative block I/O scheduling weight restored when active.

- **Type:** integer `10`-`1000`
- **Example:** `"500"`
- **Since:** unreleased
- **Providers:** Docker and Podman only.
- **Learn more:** [feature page](/features/scale-mode/block-io/)

## `sablier.idle.blkio-weight-device`

The per-device I/O scheduling weight applied when idle.

- **Type:** `path:weight` list
- **Example:** `"/dev/sda:100"`
- **Since:** unreleased
- **Providers:** Docker only; per-device limits require daemon API >= 1.55.
- **Learn more:** [feature page](/features/scale-mode/block-io/)

## `sablier.active.blkio-weight-device`

The per-device I/O scheduling weight restored when active.

- **Type:** `path:weight` list
- **Example:** `"/dev/sda:500"`
- **Since:** unreleased
- **Providers:** Docker only; per-device limits require daemon API >= 1.55.
- **Learn more:** [feature page](/features/scale-mode/block-io/)

## `sablier.idle.blkio-device-read-bps`

The per-device read throughput limit applied when idle (Docker byte units, e.g. `10m` = 10 MB/s).

- **Type:** `path:rate` list
- **Example:** `"/dev/sda:10m"`
- **Since:** unreleased
- **Providers:** Docker only; requires daemon API >= 1.55.
- **Learn more:** [feature page](/features/scale-mode/block-io/)

## `sablier.active.blkio-device-read-bps`

The per-device read throughput limit restored when active.

- **Type:** `path:rate` list
- **Example:** `"/dev/sda:100m"`
- **Since:** unreleased
- **Providers:** Docker only; requires daemon API >= 1.55.
- **Learn more:** [feature page](/features/scale-mode/block-io/)

## `sablier.idle.blkio-device-write-bps`

The per-device write throughput limit applied when idle.

- **Type:** `path:rate` list
- **Example:** `"/dev/sda:10m"`
- **Since:** unreleased
- **Providers:** Docker only; requires daemon API >= 1.55.
- **Learn more:** [feature page](/features/scale-mode/block-io/)

## `sablier.active.blkio-device-write-bps`

The per-device write throughput limit restored when active.

- **Type:** `path:rate` list
- **Example:** `"/dev/sda:100m"`
- **Since:** unreleased
- **Providers:** Docker only; requires daemon API >= 1.55.
- **Learn more:** [feature page](/features/scale-mode/block-io/)

## `sablier.idle.blkio-device-read-iops`

The per-device read IOPS limit applied when idle.

- **Type:** `path:iops` list
- **Example:** `"/dev/sda:100"`
- **Since:** unreleased
- **Providers:** Docker only; requires daemon API >= 1.55.
- **Learn more:** [feature page](/features/scale-mode/block-io/)

## `sablier.active.blkio-device-read-iops`

The per-device read IOPS limit restored when active.

- **Type:** `path:iops` list
- **Example:** `"/dev/sda:1000"`
- **Since:** unreleased
- **Providers:** Docker only; requires daemon API >= 1.55.
- **Learn more:** [feature page](/features/scale-mode/block-io/)

## `sablier.idle.blkio-device-write-iops`

The per-device write IOPS limit applied when idle.

- **Type:** `path:iops` list
- **Example:** `"/dev/sda:100"`
- **Since:** unreleased
- **Providers:** Docker only; requires daemon API >= 1.55.
- **Learn more:** [feature page](/features/scale-mode/block-io/)

## `sablier.active.blkio-device-write-iops`

The per-device write IOPS limit restored when active.

- **Type:** `path:iops` list
- **Example:** `"/dev/sda:1000"`
- **Since:** unreleased
- **Providers:** Docker only; requires daemon API >= 1.55.
- **Learn more:** [feature page](/features/scale-mode/block-io/)
